title: How do they repair Evangelions
wikitext:
[]: How do they repair Evangelions?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Jun 05, 2009 9:22 am Since they are biological constructs and not robots, how do they fix missing limbs and whatnot? The manga mentioned the Evas could eventually regenerate limbs and heal injuries, but the anime never did explain how they did it. Was this just a plot hole, or did I miss something?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jun 05, 2009 11:17 am Yui proves in ep2 she can regrow limbs from other people's limbs, so presumably all Nerv really has to do is jam mass quantities of PWM against the injury site. They could be doing 'repairs' for quite a while just with the salvaged remains of Shamshel and Ramiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jun 05, 2009 11:26 am They coat the rubber science with a helping of plausible terminology episode 22' wrote: WOMAN (OFF): Mitosis in Unit 02's left arm has cleared the target value. MAN (OFF): Necrosis is currently under point zero five percent. WOMAN (OFF): The apoptosis process is operating normally. WOMAN (OFF): Unit 00's morphogenetic system is currently stable.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Fri Jun 05, 2009 12:08 pm yea basically they force grow back the limbs, if the damage is too much they use other limbs like they did for eva 05 and 06

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Fri Jun 05, 2009 12:18 pm Well, they don't exactly say where they get the extra flesh from when the evas are damaged beyond the "Hayflick Limit". I always thought that it was only inorganic parts that they took from other evas.

[]: Re: How do they repair Evangelions?             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Jun 05, 2009 12:20 pm CyberXIII wrote: The manga mentioned the Evas could eventually regenerate limbs and heal injuries,...

[]: Re: How do they repair Evangelions?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Jun 05, 2009 7:46 pm SaltyJoe wrote: CyberXIII wrote: The manga mentioned the Evas could eventually regenerate limbs and heal injuries,...

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Fri Jun 05, 2009 8:11 pm I'm pretty sure that the evas can't just grow a new head. From ep 22: It looks like they are attaching a completed head to Unit 02. Armor and all, as strange as that may seem. Also: Gendo has access to stem cells from the Adam embryo. They can use those to grow eva tissue.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Fri Jun 05, 2009 8:59 pm thats from when they had to use parts from 05 and 06 after the zeruel battle

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Fri Jun 05, 2009 9:04 pm Yes, but we know that the Eva 02 had a special head (four eyes), while all other production evas seem to have only two eyes. So they can't just substitute one head for another, can they? They still had Nigouki's head lying around, so they might have just repaired and re-attached it.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Jun 06, 2009 6:31 am Lets go for a combined explanation: the regenerative abilities mentioned in the manga (and sometimes showcased by Eva-01, at least, in the series) are the reason why they can simply reattach the various parts of the Evangelions onto their bodies. As NemZ mentioned earlier, any sort of PWM might do the trick, when they don't have a specific limb handy (Eva-02 lost one of it's legs in Episode 10 if i recall correctly, so they ave to have to have some spear parts/PWM lying around to replace that). After all, Eva-01 was able to use Zeruel's arm to regrow her own in Episode 19 (i know it was a special case and she is special too, but still). Maybe the Evas could regenerate anything lost in combat given enough time: reattaching the lost parts is just faster. In short, i think the bodily damages can be repaired by simply rubbing PWM on the wounds or gluing anything that was lost back onto the Eva's body. Most of the repairs were probably required for the electronics inside the Evas and the restraints. @Baz: Good call on growing tissue from Adam. I wonder whether there is anything in the Evangelion graveyard they could use for spares? 


[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sat Jun 06, 2009 11:07 am @SaltyJoe: You've provided a very complete picture, but I think that you are still in error on one point: SaltyJoe wrote: Maybe the Evas could regenerate anything lost in combat given enough time: reattaching the lost parts is just faster.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sat Jun 06, 2009 2:12 pm the particle wave matter has been shown be very clay like, they can probably mold it into whatever they want

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jun 06, 2009 2:18 pm Baz wrote: @SaltyJoe: You've provided a very complete picture, but I think that you are still in error on one point: SaltyJoe wrote: Maybe the Evas could regenerate anything lost in combat given enough time: reattaching the lost parts is just faster.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sat Jun 06, 2009 5:00 pm Axell wrote: the particle wave matter has been shown be very clay like, they can probably mold it into whatever they want

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Jun 06, 2009 7:06 pm Quantum magic.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Jun 10, 2009 9:11 am Baz wrote: The show specifically mentions the Hayflick limit , which is a limit to how many times a differentiated cell can divide. This places a strict limit on how much flesh can be regenerated if you don't have stem cells.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jun 10, 2009 3:11 pm SaltyJoe wrote: This statement is truer than i thought.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jun 10, 2009 3:53 pm Baz wrote: I'm pretty sure that the evas can't just grow a new head. From ep 22: It looks like they are attaching a completed head to Unit 02. Armor and all, as strange as that may seem.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Wed Jun 10, 2009 3:59 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Aren't they just reattaching it from when it got decapitated by Zereul? Did I miss something?
summary:
[]: How do they repair Evangelions?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Jun 05, 2009 9:22 am Since they are biological constructs and not robots, how do they fix missing limbs and whatnot? The manga mentioned the Evas could eventually regenerate limbs and heal injuries, but the anime never did explain how they did it. Was this just a plot hole, or did I miss something?
